seed_problem: spectral-radius minimization by edge deletion on a contact graph with overlapping planted dense cores
mutation: composite: minimize the adjacency spectral radius under an exact-k edge-deletion budget with a connectivity side-constraint, on inputs planted with several near-degenerate dense cores so the certifying principal eigenvector migrates under the solver's own edits (minimax-over-cores objective)
theme: cutting contact links to suppress an epidemic threshold
title: Quarantine Cuts on a Drifting Contact Graph
strategies: trivial: delete the k least-central (smallest endpoint degree-product) non-tree edges -> barely moves lambda (the checker baseline; ratio ~0.1)

greedy (the trap): rank edges by x_u*x_v of the ORIGINAL principal eigenvector and delete the top k -> dumps the whole budget into the dominant core; once it drops below a secondary core the top eigenvalue migrates and the remaining cuts are wasted

strong: iterated re-ranking / certificate-following -- delete in small batches, recompute the principal eigenvector after each batch so cuts track the drifting eigenvector, water-filling suppression across all cores to minimize the max residual core spectral radius
restarted local search: seed with a balanced allocation across cores, then swap individual cuts to greedily reduce the recomputed Rayleigh quotient
innovation_hook: the certifying principal eigenvector rotates under the solver's own edits, so the real problem is a minimax over which community hosts the top eigenvalue after intervention
